checkpoints.trigger: before implementing the publish endpoints and their UI
checkpoints.question: Publishing to eBay is the pipeline's most outward-facing action (a real listing on a real marketplace once production flips). The UI now gains publish controls. Options for v1: (a) full two-step in the UI — prepare (dry-run payload shown) then a separate confirm click calls publish apply; fastest daily flow, marker re-validation still applies server-side, but a listing goes live from two clicks in a browser tab; (b) UI prepare-only — the UI shows the exact payload and a copy-able CLI apply command, actual apply stays terminal-only for one more cycle (zero new blast radius, but keeps the CLI dependency the console exists to remove); (c) two-step in UI plus a cooling gate — confirm button disabled for N seconds while the payload is displayed, and the confirm records payload-digest so a changed row voids it (mirrors the run_stage4/approve marker two-step semantics in UI form). Morley runs this solo behind Tailscale; sandbox now, production later. Which boundary for v1?
checkpoints.on_synthesis: implement exactly the chosen boundary in plugin_api.py and the UI, preserving server-side marker re-validation regardless, with tests for the gate behavior

checkpoints.trigger: before implementing the approve form's acknowledgment control
checkpoints.question: CLI approve of a non-high/high-confidence listing requires --ack-low-confidence (council decision, audit-recorded). In the UI, what is the equivalent conscious step? Options: (a) a checkbox 'I acknowledge the appraisal confidence is not high' that arms the Approve button — one click, low friction, but checkbox-blindness is real; (b) the confidence block expands and the operator must type a short reason ('why approving anyway') that is stored alongside acknowledged_low_confidence in the approval jsonb — a real audit artifact, slightly slower; (c) tiered — checkbox for medium/low, typed reason required only for unknown or authenticity-flagged items. The point of the gate is preventing ACCIDENTAL approval of uncertain appraisals ('I'd rather I-don't-know than wrong'), not adding ceremony to deliberate ones. Which UX?
checkpoints.on_synthesis: implement exactly the chosen control in the UI and pass its artifacts through the approve endpoint into the approval jsonb, with tests covering each confidence tier's path
